era: 1990-present. Hard Rock. E=7 -- riff-driven, dynamic verse-chorus, Kevin Martin tenor, Seattle.

theory_nodes.concept: Return appearance: degree 223 (post-grunge) → degree 267 (hard rock). Genre reframing as engine operation. Same band, different frame. 44-degree gap. Post-grunge positions in a moment; hard rock positions in a lineage.
theory_nodes.description: Genre reframing across return appearances. The hard rock lineage vs. the post-grunge moment.

theory_nodes.concept: Maverick Records / Madonna connection. Seattle hard rock band on Madonna's label. 1993 music industry context. 4 million copies. The commercial infrastructure of the grunge moment deployed across a wide range.
theory_nodes.description: The improbable label pairing. Madonna's Maverick + Seattle hard rock = 4 million copies.

theory_nodes.concept: Kevin Martin as hard rock vocal instrument. Wide-ranging tenor capable of melodic restraint and full-power delivery. Dynamic hard rock vocal style in the tradition of the genre's great vocalists.
theory_nodes.description: The voice as the defining instrument. Hard rock vocal tradition in Seattle context.

cross_references.relationship: FIRST APPEARANCE. 44-degree gap. Genre reframing: post-grunge moment → hard rock lineage. Same band, different analytical frame, different energy level.
Energy step down: E=8 (crossover thrash) → E=7 (hard rock). Dynamic contrast immediately preceding this entry.
E=8 returns. Proto-punk, Tacoma. Energy rises after Candlebox's E=7 hard rock.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 (Candlebox) + E=8 (mudslide). Asymmetric. The mudslide is the final and highest-energy event of the Geophysics arc. Candlebox's hard rock is high energy; the Oso mudslide is geophysical maximum for the arc.

milestones: POST-GRUNGE TO HARD ROCK GENRE SHIFT
